taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about the lungs and trachea.
textField:
The trachea is *10* cm long and extends from *C6* to the level of T4/T5, where it bifurcates at the *carina*. The trachea has 16-20 *C-shaped* cartilage rings that are open *posteriorly*, bridged by the *trachealis* muscle.

The right main bronchus is *wider*, shorter, and more *vertical* than the left, making it the common site for foreign body aspiration. The right lung has *three* lobes and *10* bronchopulmonary segments, while the left lung has *two* lobes.

The *hilum* of the lung is the area where structures enter and leave. The pulmonary *ligament* is a double fold of pleura that extends below the hilum, allowing for expansion of the pulmonary veins.

Needle insertion for chest drainage should be over the *upper* border of the lower rib to avoid the intercostal *neurovascular* bundle, which runs in the costal groove along the inferior border of each rib.

The right lung has *horizontal* and oblique fissures, while the left lung has only the *oblique* fissure. The *lingula* of the left lung is the anatomical equivalent of the middle lobe of the right lung.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Review the details you missed.
